original_hypothesis.title: withdrawRemainingTitn Snapshot Race: First Caller Determines Distribution for All Users
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The first caller of withdrawRemainingTitn() after 360 days sets remainingTitnAfter1Year and initialTotalClaimable permanently. These snapshot values determine the proportional bonus for ALL subsequent users. If the contract's TITN balance is manipulated before the first call (e.g., by someone sending TITN directly to the contract or owner withdrawing), the snapshot captures a distorted state. While donation doesn't benefit the donor (analysis shows net-negative), the owner or a coordinated entity could withdraw TITN just before the snapshot to reduce everyone's proportional bonus.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The one-shot snapshot mechanism creates a critical window where balance manipulation affects all future distributions. The snapshot includes actual TITN balance (balanceOf) which can be influenced externally, while totalTitnClaimable is deterministic at that point (no deposits/claims possible after 360d).

proof_of_signal.pattern: Lines 107-111: `if (remainingTitnAfter1Year == 0) { remainingTitnAfter1Year = currentRemainingTitn; initialTotalClaimable = totalTitnClaimable; }` — One-shot initialization based on current state, permanent for all users.

preconditions: remainingTitnAfter1Year not yet initialized (no prior call)
Entity can influence contract's TITN balance before first call (e.g., direct TITN transfer or owner withdrawal)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: remainingTitnAfter1Year = titn.balanceOf(address(this)) — uses live balance, not tracked deposits
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Send extra TITN to contract (inflates snapshot) or owner withdraws TITN (deflates snapshot) before first call
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Donation analysis: user with X/T share donating D gets X*D/T back, net loss D*(1-X/T). Not profitable for individuals. Owner withdrawal: reduces all users' proportional shares.

economic_questions: Can any non-owner entity manipulate the balance for profit at snapshot time?
What is the maximum financial impact of snapshot manipulation?
Does the contract have protections against direct TITN sends?
validation_requirements: Simulate: send extra TITN before snapshot, verify increased proportional shares for all
Simulate: owner withdraws TITN before snapshot, verify decreased proportional shares
Calculate maximum redistribution impact per unit of balance manipulation

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Varies; donation requires significant TITN for meaningful impact
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Net negative for donation attacks; owner withdrawal reduces all users' bonuses
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Not profitable for non-owner; owner gains withdrawn amount at users' expense

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None for owner; significant TITN for donation attack
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately before first withdrawRemainingTitn call after 360 days
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Proportional reduction in bonus for all unclaimed users if owner withdraws

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users with unclaimed TITN at 360-day mark
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Reduced proportional bonus; magnitude depends on manipulation amount
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Up to the full unclaimed TITN bonus pool

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Expected contract balance at 360d = TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed; any deviation indicates manipulation
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify snapshot uses balanceOf (manipulable) rather than tracked accounting

validation_result.impact_summary: The first caller of withdrawRemainingTitn() permanently sets distribution parameters using a live balanceOf() call. Owner can withdraw TITN before this snapshot to reduce all users' proportional bonuses, or anyone can inflate it via direct token transfer.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The withdrawRemainingTitn() function at MergeTgt.sol lines 107-111 contains a one-shot initialization that uses titn.balanceOf(address(this)) — a live, externally-manipulable value — to permanently set remainingTitnAfter1Year and initialTotalClaimable. These two values determine the proportional bonus for ALL subsequent users who call withdrawRemainingTitn(). The defect is using balanceOf (which reflects actual token balance including any direct sends or owner withdrawals) instead of internal accounting (e.g., TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed). This creates two manipulation vectors: (1) Owner calls withdraw(titn, amount) before the snapshot to reduce remainingTitnAfter1Year, thereby reducing unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable and shrinking all users' proportional shares; (2) Anyone can send TITN directly to the contract to inflate the snapshot. After 360 days, no legitimate operations can change totalTitnClaimable (onTokenTransfer and claimTitn are both blocked), so the internal accounting is stable — but the balance is not.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 105-111 of MergeTgt.sol clearly show the one-shot snapshot using balanceOf. Line 106: `uint256 currentRemainingTitn = titn.balanceOf(address(this));` — this is the manipulable input. Lines 107-111: the if-block permanently sets both snapshot values. Line 118: `uint256 unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable;` — this subtraction determines the bonus pool, and its result is directly affected by any balance manipulation. The withdraw() function at line 56 allows the owner to extract any amount of any token with no restriction, confirming the owner manipulation vector.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: remainingTitnAfter1Year is set from titn.balanceOf(address(this)) which can be manipulated by (1) owner calling withdraw() to reduce the balance, or (2) anyone sending TITN directly to the contract to inflate the balance. This permanently determines the bonus distribution for all users. Internal accounting (TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed) should be used instead.

Owner can withdraw TITN from the contract at any time, including right before the 360-day snapshot. No restriction prevents withdrawing user-claimable TITN, enabling the owner to reduce the bonus pool before the snapshot is taken.

calling_sequence: 1. Owner calls withdraw(titn, amount) at MergeTgt.sol:55 to drain some TITN before 360 days elapse
2. After 360 days, any user calls withdrawRemainingTitn() at MergeTgt.sol:100
3. Time check passes at line 103 (block.timestamp - launchTime >= 360 days)
4. Line 106: currentRemainingTitn = titn.balanceOf(address(this)) — captures reduced balance
5. Line 107: remainingTitnAfter1Year == 0 is true (first call), enters initialization block
6. Line 109: remainingTitnAfter1Year = currentRemainingTitn (reduced value set permanently)
8. Line 118: unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable (smaller than intended)
9. Line 119: userProportionalShare calculated with deflated unclaimedTitn
10. All subsequent callers use the same deflated remainingTitnAfter1Year, receiving reduced bonuses
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path starts at line 106 where balanceOf is used instead of internal accounting. The owner can reduce this balance via withdraw() (line 55-58) at any time before the snapshot. After the one-shot initialization at lines 107-111, the snapshot is permanent — remainingTitnAfter1Year will never be 0 again (assuming balance was non-zero), so the if-block never re-executes. All subsequent users compute their proportional share at line 119 using the deflated unclaimedTitn value from line 118.

deployment_assumptions_checked: TITN_ARB deposit of 173,700,000 TITN is made to MergeTgt
config_validation.deployment_evidence: arbitrumSetup.ts confirms TITN_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT = '173700000000000000000000000' deposited. No restrictions on withdraw() are added post-deployment. Owner retains full control.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Owner manipulation scenario: Before 360 days elapse, the owner calls withdraw(titn, X) to extract some TITN. After 360 days, the first user calls withdrawRemainingTitn(). The snapshot captures (TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed - X) as remainingTitnAfter1Year instead of the correct (TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed). The bonus pool unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable is reduced by X. All users' proportional shares are reduced proportionally. The owner has effectively extracted X TITN from the bonus pool. Donation scenario: An external party sends D TITN directly to the contract before the snapshot. The bonus pool is inflated by D, giving all remaining users slightly larger proportional shares at the donor's expense (net negative for donor). While the donation attack is not profitable for the attacker, the owner withdrawal attack IS profitable for the owner at users' expense.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The owner can extract TITN from the contract before the snapshot, reducing the proportional bonus for all users who have not yet claimed. The maximum impact is the entire unclaimed bonus pool (TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed - totalTitnClaimable at the 360-day mark). If the owner withdraws enough TITN to make remainingTitnAfter1Year < initialTotalClaimable, the subtraction at line 118 underflows (Solidity 0.8+ reverts), permanently DoS-ing all post-year claims — a more severe outcome covered by INV-STATE-003.
recommended_mitigations: Replace titn.balanceOf(address(this)) with internal accounting: remainingTitnAfter1Year = TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed. This eliminates external balance manipulation.
Restrict withdraw() to prevent withdrawing TITN (or at minimum, prevent withdrawing more than the surplus above totalTitnClaimable).
Consider initializing the snapshot in a dedicated owner function or automatically at the 360-day boundary rather than relying on the first user call.
Add a safeguard: require(remainingTitnAfter1Year >= initialTotalClaimable) after initialization to prevent underflow scenarios.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy MergeTgt with TITN and TGT tokens. 2. Owner deposits TITN_ARB. 3. Set launch time, set locked status to OneWay. 4. Have users deposit TGT via transferAndCall during the first 90 days. 5. Advance time to day 359. 6. Owner calls withdraw(titn, X) to extract some TITN. 7. Advance time to day 361. 8. First user calls withdrawRemainingTitn(). 9. Verify remainingTitnAfter1Year = TITN_ARB - totalTitnClaimed - X (reduced). 10. Verify all users' proportional shares are reduced by comparing against a control run without the owner withdrawal.
uncertainties: The severity depends on whether the owner is considered trusted. In a trust-minimized protocol, this is a critical defect. In an owner-trusted model, the snapshot manipulation via direct TITN sends (by non-owner) is lower impact since it only benefits other users at the donor's expense.
The exact financial impact depends on how much TITN remains unclaimed at the 360-day mark, which depends on user behavior.
additional_testing_needed: Test owner withdrawal of varying amounts before the snapshot and verify the impact on all users' proportional shares
Test direct TITN transfer to the contract before the snapshot and verify the inflation effect
Test edge case where owner drains ALL TITN, leaving balance = 0, and verify the perpetual re-initialization failure (remainingTitnAfter1Year stays 0)
Test concurrent calls in the same block to verify first-caller determinism under MEV conditions
